subject: English
title: The Best Christmas Present in the World

questions.text: Where did the author find the roll-top desk?
options: In a junk shop in Bridport
questions.explanation: The author found the 19th-century oak desk in a junk shop in Bridport.

questions.text: What was the condition of the roll-top desk when the author bought it?
options: In bad condition, scorched on one side
questions.explanation: The desk was in a clumsy condition with burn marks down one side.

questions.text: When did the author begin working on the desk?
questions.explanation: He started repairing the desk on Christmas Eve.

questions.text: What did the author find in the secret drawer?
questions.explanation: Inside the secret drawer, he found a small black tin box taped with a letter.

questions.text: Who was the letter addressed to?
questions.explanation: The letter was addressed to Mrs. Jim Macpherson at 12 Copper Beeches, Bridport.

questions.text: What date was written on the letter found in the desk?
questions.explanation: The letter was written on December 26, 1914.

questions.text: Who was Jim Macpherson in the story?
options: A captain of the British army
questions.explanation: Jim Macpherson was a captain in the British army during World War I.

questions.text: Before joining the army, what was Jim Macpherson's profession?
questions.explanation: Jim was a school teacher from Dorset in the west of England.

questions.text: Who initiated the Christmas truce in the letter?
questions.explanation: The Germans (Fritzes) first waved a white flag from the trenches.

questions.text: What did the German officer, Hans Wolf, play in the orchestra before the war?
questions.explanation: Hans Wolf mentioned he used to play the cello in an orchestra in Dusseldorf.

questions.text: Which book by Thomas Hardy did Jim and Hans discuss?
questions.explanation: They talked about Bathsheba, Gabriel Oak, and the book 'Far from the Madding Crowd'.

questions.text: What food and drink did the soldiers share during the truce?
questions.explanation: The soldiers shared German schnapps and canned sausage along with British rum.

questions.text: How did the soldiers spend time together during the truce?
questions.explanation: The Tommies and Fritzes played a football match in no man's land.

questions.text: Who won the football match described in the letter?
questions.explanation: The Germans won two goals to one.

questions.text: What did Hans Wolf and Jim Macpherson agree about resolving wars?
options: Wars should be resolved by a football match
questions.explanation: They agreed that a football match is a better way to resolve conflict because no one dies and no children are orphaned.

questions.text: What was the name of Jim's wife?
questions.explanation: Her name was Connie Macpherson.

questions.text: What happened to Connie's house?
questions.explanation: Her house had caught fire, likely caused by candles she used instead of electricity.

questions.text: Where was Connie staying when the author went to find her?
options: In the ruins of her house
questions.explanation: She was staying in a nursing home on the Dorchester Road.

questions.text: How old was Connie when the author visited her?
questions.explanation: The story mentions she was a hundred and one years old.

questions.text: Who did Connie mistake the author for?
questions.explanation: Due to her age and memory, she thought the author was Jim returning home for Christmas.

questions.text: What did Connie call 'the best Christmas present in the world'?
questions.explanation: She believed Jim had finally come home as promised, which was the best gift for her.

questions.text: Which county in England is Bridport located in?
questions.explanation: Bridport is in Dorset, which is where Jim was from.

questions.text: Why did Connie use candles instead of electricity?
options: She thought electricity was too expensive
questions.explanation: The narrator mentions she always thought electricity was too expensive.

questions.text: What was the atmosphere in No Man's Land during the truce?
questions.explanation: Despite being enemies, the soldiers shared food, laughter, and games.

questions.text: What did the author do with the letter at the end?
questions.explanation: He felt a sense of duty to return the letter to its owner.

questions.text: What is 'No Man's Land'?
options: The area between two opposing armies' trenches
questions.explanation: It refers to the disputed or unoccupied ground between the front lines of two armies.

questions.text: What was the significance of the white flag mentioned by Jim?
questions.explanation: The German soldier waved a white flag to indicate they wanted to stop fighting for Christmas.

questions.text: Hans Wolf said he had never been to Dorset but knew it from:
questions.explanation: He knew England and Dorset from reading books in English at school.

questions.text: What was the color of the German soldiers' uniforms?
questions.explanation: The Germans wore grey coats (Fritzes) while the British wore khaki.

questions.text: Jim promised to be home by which occasion in his letter?
questions.explanation: He wrote that he expected to be back by next Christmas.

questions.text: What did the author find difficult about opening the desk drawer?
options: It was stuck tight due to water and fire damage
questions.explanation: The last drawer was stuck fast and required force to open.

questions.text: Who is the author of 'The Best Christmas Present in the World'?
questions.explanation: The story was written by the famous British author Michael Morpurgo.

questions.text: What was the feeling of the author when he found the letter?
questions.explanation: He felt that 'scruples' (guilt) should have stopped him from reading it, but curiosity won.

questions.text: The phrase 'Happy Christmas, Tommy' was shouted by:
questions.explanation: The Germans shouted this to the British soldiers to start the truce.

questions.text: What does the story highlight about war?
options: Soldiers on both sides want peace and family
It is the only way to win
questions.explanation: The story shows that the common soldier values humanity and peace over national conflict.

questions.text: How did the author know the address of Connie?
options: It was written on the envelope in the tin box
He looked it up in a phone book
questions.explanation: The address was clearly written on the envelope inside the desk.

questions.text: In the letter, what was the 'marzipan'?
options: A sweet covering on a cake
questions.explanation: Marzipan is a sugar and almond paste used on Christmas cakes, which Jim said was the best he'd tasted.

questions.text: Which sport was used to symbolize a peaceful alternative to war?
questions.explanation: Football was the game played by both sides in the story.

questions.text: What did the author find in the middle drawer of the desk?
questions.explanation: The text describes the autor opening the first and second drawers, but the secret box was specifically in the secret drawer beneath a fake bottom.

questions.text: What is the mood at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: It is touching because Connie is happy, but sad because the reader knows Jim never actually returned.
